broad_generalizability.summary: The broad prompt unexpectedly produced a strong audit: it loaded the model, reproduced predictions, computed Morgan Tanimoto nearest-neighbor similarity, Murcko scaffold overlap, descriptor checks, and similarity-binned errors. It did not organize the work as explicit reusable property graphs or validate spectral thresholds as completely as /spectra.

distance_from_train.summary: The explicit distance prompt produced a clean Morgan Tanimoto distance curve and validated that distance quintiles reduced nearest-neighbor similarity and scaffold overlap. It was focused and technically solid, but less multi-axis and less artifact-complete than /spectra.

spectra_protocol.summary: The /spectra prompt produced the most complete audit: fingerprint, scaffold/skeleton, descriptor, functional-group, and target-support axes; explicit bipartite, membership, kNN, and target-support graphs; threshold-level overlap validation; and multiple performance-vs-novelty curves.

shared_core_finding.finding: All three conditions found limited generalization: the model predicts well near training-like molecules but error increases with lower train-test molecular similarity and target/support novelty.

distance_control_result: The explicit distance-from-train prompt already captured the main spectral curve, so /spectra is not needed merely to make an agent plot error against train distance on this molecular task.
spectra_incremental_value: The /spectra condition added value by enforcing a fuller audit standard: multiple axes, explicit property graphs, threshold-level overlap validation, rejection of scaffold novelty as insufficient for this split, and reusable audit structure.
claim_supported: /spectra improves protocol completeness beyond a direct distance-from-train prompt in this single molecular run.
claim_not_supported: This run does not support the claim that broad or distance-prompted agents cannot discover the core spectral generalization failure.
caveats: This is one task with one agent sample per condition.
All conditions were allowed to use the same Python/RDKit-capable environment, which made the broad condition unusually strong.
The hard-blind artifact still includes y_true, so target-support failure is visible after prediction labels are inspected.
The value claim now needs replication across tasks where broad prompts do not naturally infer the right domain distance.
